opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I scratched this bump on my forehead with a towel, and it has been dripping blood for two hours straight no matter how hard I press on it.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The bleeding won't stop with pressure and it's soaked through a heap of tissues — I'm home on my own and getting worried by the amount of blood on my face.

symptom_narrative.onset: the bleeding started this evening right after I dried my face with a towel and caught the bump
symptom_narrative.duration: about two hours of continuous oozing
symptom_narrative.character: steady dripping/oozing of blood from a lump on my forehead; the lump itself has never hurt
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: not painful, but it just keeps bleeding and it looks alarming
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: a lump high up on the right side of my forehead
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since I knocked it — eases a bit when I press hard then starts again when I let go
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: firm pressure slows it a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: letting go of the pressure, or touching/wiping it
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no dizziness, no feeling faint, no fever, no feeling unwell otherwise
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the lump has been there slowly getting bigger for about six months but it has never bled like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't settle — I'm just standing at the sink holding a towel to my head

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a slow-growing, painless small bump on the right forehead that started as a little shiny spot about six months ago and has gradually got bigger
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: first noticed roughly six months ago; never had it looked at

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; spends a lot of time outdoors and has had plenty of sun exposure over the years

communication_style.typical_message_style: distressed about the visible facial bleeding and being alone

objective_data_offered.value: a shiny, pale, raised lump about the size of a pea on the upper right forehead with a small crater/sore in the middle that is slowly oozing blood
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-held camera in poor bathroom lighting; fine surface detail cannot be assessed over video

objective_data_offered.value: the bleeding has soaked through several tissues over about two hours and firm pressure isn't fully stopping it
I don't feel dizzy, faint or lightheaded, and I don't have a fever
